rxn_class_summary: ArI-mediated electrochemical aziridination of electron-deficient alkenes using acetyl hydrazide-type N-source in an undivided cell
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of ArI to ArI(OAc)2 (hypervalent iodine, I(III)) which serves as the active mediator; cathodic reduction generates H2 and regenerates acetate, closing the electron cycle

key_intermediates.name: ArI(OAc)(NHFg) (iodine(III) species with I-O and I-N bonds, intermediate B)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArI to ArI(OAc)2 (A), which exchanges an OAc for the N-nucleophile (Fg-NH2) to give iodine(III) intermediate B bearing I-O and I-N bonds; B partitions into acetoxyamine 6 (releasing ArI) or iodonitrene 7 (releasing HOAc), and both deliver nitrogen to the alkene to give the aziridine in a stepwise manner. At the cathode, protons are reduced to H2, regenerating acetate.

probe_reagents.name: HRMS detection of reaction mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Species with masses matching compound 6 (HRMS 221.0568) and compound 7 (394.9892) both detected, supporting two coexisting pathways via acetoxyamine and iodonitrene

notes: Two parallel pathways are proposed (via acetoxyamine 6 and via iodonitrene 7), both leading to stepwise aziridination; the authors do not designate one as exclusively favored. Mechanism cites ref [10c] for the stepwise aziridination mode. No radical traps, KIE, or DFT reported on these pages.
